name: fastq-dl
description: Download FASTQ files from SRA or ENA repositories.

fastqdl.name: fastqdl
fastqdl.description: Download FASTQ files from ENA or SRA.

args.name: accession
args.description: ENA/SRA accession to query. (Study, Sample, Experiment, Run accession)

args.name: provider
args.description: Specify which provider (ENA or SRA) to use.

args.name: group_by_experiment
args.description: Group Runs by experiment accession.

args.name: group_by_sample
args.description: Group Runs by sample accession.

args.name: outdir
args.description: Directory to output downloads to.

args.name: prefix
args.description: Prefix to use for naming log files.

args.name: max_attempts
args.description: Maximum number of download attempts.

args.name: sleep
args.description: Minimum amount of time to sleep between retries (API query and download)

args.name: force
args.description: Overwrite existing files.

args.name: ignore_md5
args.description: Ignore MD5 checksums for downloaded files.

args.name: sra_lite
args.description: Set preference to SRA Lite

args.name: skip_compression
args.description: Skip pigz compression of SRA downloads.

args.name: only_provider
args.description: Only attempt download from specified provider.

args.name: only_download_metadata
args.description: Skip FASTQ downloads, and retrieve only the metadata.

args.name: cpus
args.description: Total cpus used for downloading from SRA.

args.name: silent
args.description: Only critical errors will be printed.

args.name: verbose
args.description: Print debug related text.

args.name: protocol
args.description: Protocol to use for ENA downloads.

_run_download.name: _run_download
_run_download.description:
Internal function that performs the actual download logic.

This function contains the core download logic and may raise FastqDLError
subclasses which are caught by the CLI wrapper.

Raises:
    ValidationError: If the accession is invalid.
    ProviderError: If there's an error querying ENA/SRA.
    DownloadError: If there's an error downloading files.

args.description: ENA/SRA accession to query. (Study, Sample, Experiment, Run accession)

args.description: Specify which provider (ENA or SRA) to use.

args.description: Group Runs by experiment accession.

_download_with_fallback.name: _download_with_fallback
_download_with_fallback.description:
Download FASTQs with fallback to alternate provider.

Args:
    run_info: Dictionary containing run metadata (needed for ENA download)
    run_acc: Run accession string
    outdir: Output directory
    primary_provider: 'ena' or 'sra' - which provider to try first
    only_provider: If True, do not fallback to alternate provider
    max_attempts: Maximum download attempts
    force: Overwrite existing files
    ignore_md5: Skip MD5 verification
    sleep: Sleep time between retries
    cpus: Number of CPUs for SRA download
    sra_lite: Use SRA Lite format
    protocol: Protocol for ENA downloads (ftp or https)

Returns:
    tuple: (fastqs dict or None, error string or None)

get_ena_metadata.name: get_ena_metadata
get_ena_metadata.description:
Fetch metadata from ENA.
https://docs.google.com/document/d/1CwoY84MuZ3SdKYocqssumghBF88PWxUZ/edit#heading=h.ag0eqy2wfin5

Args:
    query (str): The query to search for.
    max_attempts (int): Maximum number of query attempts. Defaults to 3.
    sleep (int): Seconds to wait between retry attempts. Defaults to 10.

Returns:
    list: Records associated with the accession.

args.name: query
args.description: The query to search for.

args.name: max_attempts
args.description: Maximum number of query attempts. Defaults to 3.

args.name: sleep
args.description: Seconds to wait between retry attempts. Defaults to 10.

ena_download.name: ena_download
ena_download.description:
Download FASTQs from ENA FTP using wget.

Args:
    run (dict): Dictionary of run info to download associated FASTQs.
    outdir (str): Directory to write FASTQs to.
    max_attempts (int, optional): Maximum number of download attempts. Defaults to 10.
    force: (bool, optional): Whether to overwrite existing files if the MD5's do not match
    ignore_md5 (bool, optional): Ignore MD5 checksums for downloaded files
    sleep (int): Minimum amount of time to sleep before retry
    protocol (str): Protocol for download (ftp or https)

Returns:
    Union[dict, str]: A dictionary of the FASTQs and their paired status, or ENA_FAILED on error.
        The dict contains:
        - r1 (str): Path to R1 FASTQ file
        - r2 (str): Path to R2 FASTQ file (empty string if single-end)
        - single_end (bool): True if single-end, False if paired-end
        - orphan (str | None): Always None for ENA (orphan reads not supported)

args.name: run
args.description: Dictionary of run info to download associated FASTQs.

args.name: outdir
args.description: Directory to write FASTQs to.

args.name: max_attempts
args.description: Maximum number of download attempts. Defaults to 10.

args.name: force
args.description: (bool, optional): Whether to overwrite existing files if the MD5's do not match

args.name: ignore_md5
args.description: Ignore MD5 checksums for downloaded files

args.name: sleep
args.description: Minimum amount of time to sleep before retry

args.name: protocol
args.description: Protocol for download (ftp or https)

download_ena_fastq.name: download_ena_fastq
download_ena_fastq.description:
Download FASTQs from ENA using FTP or HTTPS.

Args:
    ftp (str): The FTP address of the FASTQ file.
    outdir (str): Directory to download the FASTQ to.
    md5 (str): Expected MD5 checksum of the FASTQ.
    max_attempts (int, optional): Maximum number of download attempts. Defaults to 10.
    force: (bool, optional): Whether to overwrite existing files if the MD5's do not match
    ignore_md5 (bool, optional): Ignore MD5 checksums for downloaded files
    sleep (int): Minimum amount of time to sleep before retry
    protocol (str): Protocol for download (ftp or https)

Returns:
    str: Path to the downloaded FASTQ, or ENA_FAILED on error.

Raises:
    DownloadError: When download fails after max_attempts due to MD5 mismatch.

args.name: ftp
args.description: The FTP address of the FASTQ file.

args.name: outdir
args.description: Directory to download the FASTQ to.

args.name: md5
args.description: Expected MD5 checksum of the FASTQ.

args.name: max_attempts
args.description: Maximum number of download attempts. Defaults to 10.

args.name: force
args.description: (bool, optional): Whether to overwrite existing files if the MD5's do not match

args.name: ignore_md5
args.description: Ignore MD5 checksums for downloaded files

args.name: sleep
args.description: Minimum amount of time to sleep before retry

args.name: protocol
args.description: Protocol for download (ftp or https)

_is_ena_empty_response.name: _is_ena_empty_response
_is_ena_empty_response.description: Check if ENA returned HTTP 200 with no data rows.

get_run_info.name: get_run_info
get_run_info.description:
Retrieve a list of samples available from ENA.

The first attempt will be against ENA, and if that fails, SRA will be queried. This should
capture those samples not yet synced between ENA and SRA.

Args:
    accession (str): The accession to search for.
    query (str): A formatted query for ENA searches.
    provider (str): Limit queries only to the specified provider (requires only_provider be true)
    only_provider (bool): If true, limit queries to the specified provider
    max_attempts (int, optional): Maximum number of download attempts
    sleep (int): Minimum amount of time to sleep before retry

Returns:
    tuple: Records associated with the accession.

args.name: accession
args.description: The accession to search for.

args.name: query
args.description: A formatted query for ENA searches.

args.name: provider
args.description: Limit queries only to the specified provider (requires only_provider be true)

args.name: only_provider
args.description: If true, limit queries to the specified provider

args.name: max_attempts
args.description: Maximum number of download attempts

args.name: sleep
args.description: Minimum amount of time to sleep before retry

get_sra_metadata.name: get_sra_metadata
get_sra_metadata.description:
Fetch metadata from SRA.

Args:
    query (str): The accession to search for.
    max_attempts (int): Maximum number of query attempts. Defaults to 3.
    sleep (int): Seconds to wait between retry attempts. Defaults to 10.

Returns:
    list: Records associated with the accession.

args.name: query
args.description: The accession to search for.

args.name: max_attempts

args.name: sleep
args.description: Seconds to wait between retry attempts. Defaults to 10.

sra_download.name: sra_download
sra_download.description:
Download FASTQs from SRA using fasterq-dump.

Args:
    accession (str): The accession to download associated FASTQs.
    outdir (str): Directory to write FASTQs to.
    cpus (int, optional): Number of CPUs to use. Defaults to 1.
    max_attempts (int, optional): Maximum number of download attempts. Defaults to 10.
    force (bool, optional): Force overwrite of existing files
    ignore_md5 (bool, optional): Ignore MD5 checksums for downloaded files
    sleep (int, default = 10): Amount of seconds to sleep in between attempts
    sra_lite (bool, optional): If True, prefer SRA Lite downloads

Returns:
    Union[dict, str]: A dictionary of the FASTQs and their paired status, or SRA_FAILED on error.
        The dict contains:
        - r1 (str): Path to R1 FASTQ file
        - r2 (str): Path to R2 FASTQ file (empty string if single-end)
        - single_end (bool): True if single-end, False if paired-end
        - orphan (str | None): Path to orphan reads file if present (paired-end with unpaired reads)

args.name: accession
args.description: The accession to download associated FASTQs.

args.name: outdir
args.description: Directory to write FASTQs to.

args.name: cpus
args.description: Number of CPUs to use. Defaults to 1.

args.name: max_attempts
args.description: Maximum number of download attempts. Defaults to 10.

args.name: force
args.description: Force overwrite of existing files

args.name: ignore_md5
args.description: Ignore MD5 checksums for downloaded files

args.name: sleep
args.description: Amount of seconds to sleep in between attempts

args.name: sra_lite
args.description: If True, prefer SRA Lite downloads

execute.name: execute
execute.description:
Execute a command using subprocess.

Args:
    cmd (Union[str, list]): A command to execute. Can be a string (will be split with shlex)
        or a list of arguments (preferred for security).
    directory (str, optional): Set the working directory for command. Defaults to str(Path.cwd()).
    capture_stdout (bool, optional): Capture and return the STDOUT of a command. Defaults to False.
    max_attempts (int, optional): Maximum times to attempt command execution. Defaults to 1.
    is_sra (bool, optional): The command is from SRA. Defaults to False.
    sleep (int): Minimum amount of time to sleep before retry

Returns:
    str: Exit code, accepted error message, or STDOUT of command.

args.name: cmd
args.description: A command to execute. Can be a string (will be split with shlex) or a list of arguments (preferred for security).

args.name: directory
args.description: Set the working directory for command. Defaults to str(Path.cwd()).

args.name: capture_stdout
args.description: Capture and return the STDOUT of a command. Defaults to False.

args.name: max_attempts
args.description: Maximum times to attempt command execution. Defaults to 1.

args.name: is_sra
args.description: The command is from SRA. Defaults to False.

args.name: sleep
args.description: Minimum amount of time to sleep before retry

merge_runs.name: merge_runs
merge_runs.description:
Merge runs from an experiment or sample.

Args:
    runs (list): A list of FASTQs to merge.
    output (str): The final merged FASTQ.

Raises:
    FileNotFoundError: If any of the input files do not exist.

args.name: output

_all_fieldnames.name: _all_fieldnames
_all_fieldnames.description:
Collect the union of all keys across a collection of dictionaries.

This function preserves first-seen order of keys rather than sorting them,
which helps maintain stable and human-readable column ordering based on
input data appearance.

Args:
    rows (Iterable[Mapping[str, Any]]):
        An iterable of dictionary-like objects representing rows.

Returns:
    List[str]:
        A list of unique field names (keys) appearing across all rows,
        in order of first occurrence.

args.name: rows
args.description: An iterable of dictionary-like objects representing rows.

write_tsv.name: write_tsv
write_tsv.description:
Write heterogeneous dictionary data to a TSV file.

This function supports two input formats:

1. A dictionary of the form:
     {
         accession: {"r1": [...], "r2": [...]},
         ...
     }
   When `output` ends with "-run-mergers.tsv", the file will contain
   columns: accession, r1, r2. Lists are joined with ";" and missing
   values are replaced with `na_value`.

2. A list of dictionaries:
     [
         {"col1": val1, "col2": val2, ...},
         ...
     ]
   In this case, the union of all keys across rows is computed and used
   as the header. Missing keys in any row are filled with `na_value`.

Args:
    data (Union[Dict[str, Any], List[Dict[str, Any]]]):
        Either:
            - A dictionary mapping identifiers to nested dictionaries
              (used for run-mergers output), or
            - A list of row dictionaries for general TSV writing.
    output (str):
        Path to the output TSV file.
    na_value (str, optional):
        Value used to fill missing fields. Defaults to "" (empty string).

Returns:
    None

Raises:
    TypeError:
        If the provided data structure does not match expected formats.

validate_query.name: validate_query
validate_query.description:
Check that query is an accepted accession type and return the accession type. Current
accepted types are:

    Projects - PRJEB, PRJNA, PRJDA
    Studies - ERP, DRP, SRP
    BioSamples - SAMD, SAME, SAMN
    Samples - ERS, DRS, SRS
    Experiments - ERX, DRX, SRX
    Runs - ERR, DRR, SRR

Parameters:
    query (str): A string containing an accession.

Returns:
    str: A string containing the query for ENA search.

https://ena-docs.readthedocs.io/en/latest/submit/general-guide/accessions.html

args.name: query
args.description: A string containing an accession.
